description: Add/remove functions to be executed in response to callback events.

keywords.name: event
keywords.description: Adds a FISH function to be called. When the function is called depends on the following keywords: The FISH function will be called every step at the location in the cycle sequence given by f . The following keywords are available to reduce the frequency of the callback. If specified, then the function will only be called every i steps. If specified, then the function will only be called if the process indicated is active during that particular cycle. The callback will only execute if the creep and mechanical process is active. The callback will only execute if the dynamic and mechanical process is active. The callback will only execute if the fluid process is active. The callback will only execute if the mechanical process is active. The callback will only execute if the thermal process is active. The FISH function will be called in response to the event named s .

keywords.name: event
keywords.description: Removes a FISH function callback. If no further keywords are specified, the function will be removed wherever it is found. The first callback found at location f in the cycle is removed. If the function has been entered multiple times only one entry will be removed. The first callback found associated with the event s is removed. If the function has been entered multiple times only one entry will be removed.

keywords.name: event
keywords.description: Adds the FISH symbol sym to be called back by the system. The location of the callback is determined by the following keyword. Note that any parameters required by sym must be given at this time enclosed in paranthesis. A FISH function sym will be called every step at the location in the cycle sequence given by f . The following keywords are available to reduce the frequency of the callback. If specified, then the function will only be called every i steps. If specified, then the function will only be called if the process indicated is active during that particular cycle. The callback will only execute if the creep and mechanical process is active. The callback will only execute if the dynamic and mechanical process is active. The callback will only execute if the fluid process is active. The callback will only execute if the mechanical process is active. The callback will only execute if the thermal process is active. The FISH function sym will be called in response to the specified event.

keywords.name: event
keywords.description: Removes sym from the list of callbacks in the system. If no event name or location is specified then the symbol will be removed wherever it is found. The following keywords and options are available: The first instance of a callback to symbol sym found at location f in the cycle is removed. If the function has been entered multiple times only one entry will be removed. The first callback to symbol sym found associated with the event is removed. If the function has been entered multiple times only one entry will be removed.

keywords.name: kwd:fos_complete
keywords.description: A factor of safety solution has completed. Check the current solve criteria to see if the model has converged. The global.fos intrinsic will return the current factor of safety.

keywords.name: kwd:fos_start
keywords.description: A factor of safety solution has started. The global.fos intrinsic will return the current factor of safety. The model will have been restored from the initial state and automatic strength reduction applied. This can be used to customized strength reduction at a given factor of safety.

keywords.name: kwd:solve_complete
keywords.description: The solve of cycle command has terminiated. One argument available of type string. If the string is not empty this indicates the error message that halted cycling.

keywords.name: kwd:solve_ending
keywords.description: The solve of cycle command is about to terminate. Cycling has stopped and cleanup operations begun.

keywords.name: kwd:solve_master
keywords.description: The code has entered the Master section of a coupled solution.

keywords.name: kwd:solve_slave
keywords.description: The code has entered a slave section of teh copuled solution. One argument is provided indicating the name of the slave process.

keywords.name: kwd:solve_start
keywords.description: The solve or cycle command is just about to start.

keywords.name: thermal
keywords.description: Adds the FISH symbol sym to be called back by the system. The location of the callback is determined by the following keyword. See the section FISH Callback Events for a discussion of FISH callbacks. A FISH function sym will be called every step at the location in the cycle sequence given by f . The following keywords are available to reduce the frequency of the callback. The FISH function sym will be called in response to the specified event. If specified, then the function will only be called every i steps. Cannot be used with the interval-time keyword. If specified, then the function will only be called every f time units of the process specified with the process keyword. If no process is specified then the mechanical process is used. Cannot be used with the interval keyword. If specified, then the function will only be called if the process indicated is active during that particular cycle. The callback will only execute if the creep and mechanical process is active. The callback will only execute if the dynamic and mechanical process is active. The callback will only execute if the fluid process is active. The callback will only execute if the mechanical process is active. The callback will only execute if the thermal process is active.

keywords.name: event
keywords.description: Removes sym from the list of callbacks in the system. If no event name or location is specified then the symbol will be removed wherever it is found. The following keywords and options are available: The first instance of a callback to symbol sym found at location f in the cycle is removed. If the function has been entered multiple times only one entry will be removed. The first callback to symbol sym found associated with the event is removed. If the function has been entered multiple times only one entry will be removed.
